ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for 'Fish Set' as a composite Indian-Singaporean set meal; mapped to the closest parent class 'fish dish' (FOODON_00002473).
ontology.foodex2_description: Fish, cooked — used as the closest FoodEx2 anchor for a composite fish-based set meal with curry, dal, pulao and naan.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for a composite Indian fish set meal. A0CKP (cooked fish) is used as the primary anchor; the rice and bread components are not separately coded here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this composite Indian fish set meal dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Fish Set serving is approximately 650 g and includes a portion of fish curry, dal tadka, saffron pulao (rice), and butter naan, as typically served at Singaporean Indian restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Fish Set — HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient profile
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95 for this exact dish entry. Saturated fat (2.31 g/100 g), trans fat (0.08 g/100 g), and vitamin C (1.54 mg/100 g) were reported in the input record at confidence 0.70 from a secondary platform source; these values are retained as the best available supplementary data and are flagged as lower-confidence estimates pending verification against an approved primary source. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and remaining micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — analogous estimate for mixed Indian rice-based set meal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a composite Fish Set (fish curry + dal tadka + saffron pulao + butter naan). GI estimated at 68 based on the weighted contribution of the primary carbohydrate components: white basmati rice/pulao (GI ~58–65, University of Sydney GI Database) and plain naan/white wheat bread (GI ~70–75, University of Sydney GI Database). The dal component (lentils, GI ~29, University of Sydney GI Database) moderates the overall GI slightly. A blended estimate of 68 (medium) is applied. Glycemic load per 650 g serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (68 × 141.05) / 100 ≈ 95.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 402 mg/100 g with insufficient potassium (185 mg/100 g) and fibre (1.5 g/100 g) offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and sambal, ask for condiments on the side, and consider low-sodium soy sauce alternatives, as the sodium content of 402 mg/100 g (approximately 2,613 mg per 650 g serving) substantially exceeds the 1,500–2,000 mg daily target for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate load (21.7 g/100 g) from white rice pulao and naan with low dietary fibre (1.5 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request brown rice or wholegrain naan to increase fibre and lower the glycaemic response, reduce the rice and naan portion while increasing the fish and dal portions, and avoid added sugar in sauces, as the estimated GI of 68 and a glycaemic load of approximately 96 per full serving represent a significant postprandial glucose challenge.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated total fat (16.2 g/100 g) and sodium (402 mg/100 g); mitigated by fish-derived omega-3 fatty acids and low saturated fat (2.31 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease benefit from the omega-3 fatty acids provided by the fish component, which support anti-inflammatory and lipid-lowering effects; however, the high sodium content (approximately 2,613 mg per serving) and total fat load require portion control, reduced condiment use, and preference for oily fish such as mackerel to maximise cardioprotective benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Fish omega-3 fatty acids and anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, curry powder); low saturated fat (2.31 g/100 g)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The fish curry component provides omega-3 fatty acids that reduce joint inflammation, and turmeric in the curry powder offers additional anti-inflammatory benefit; patients with nightshade sensitivity should request reduced or omitted chilli/sambal, and sodium should be minimised to reduce RA-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Set is a Singaporean Indian set meal consisting of fish curry, dal tadka (tempered lentils), saffron pulao (fragrant saffron-infused basmati rice), and butter naan (leavened flatbread cooked with butter). The dish is a composite plate meal commonly served at Indian restaurants and hawker stalls in Singapore, typically at lunch and dinner. The fish component — often mackerel, snapper, or pomfret — is cooked in a spiced curry sauce containing turmeric, cumin, coriander, chilli, and coconut milk or tomato base, giving it a rich, aromatic, mildly spicy flavour. The dal tadka is prepared by cooking yellow lentils and finishing with a tempering of ghee, cumin seeds, garlic, and dried chilli, producing a creamy, earthy side dish. The saffron pulao is fragrant and mildly sweet, while the butter naan provides a soft, slightly charred, buttery bread component. Nutritionally, the dish provides 277 kcal per 100 g, with 11 g protein, 16.2 g fat, and 21.7 g carbohydrate. Sodium is 402 mg/100 g, making it a moderate-to-high sodium dish. The fish provides omega-3 fatty acids beneficial for cardiovascular and joint health. The dish is halal-certified in most Singaporean Indian establishments. Clinically, the refined carbohydrate base (white rice and naan) and moderate sodium content require caution for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Requesting brown rice, wholegrain naan, and reduced condiments can improve the nutritional profile significantly.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database at 0.95 confidence for an exact dish match. Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C are retained from the input at lower confidence (0.70) and documented accordingly in notes and nutrient_source. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Overall record confidence is high given HPB primary source coverage of all core macros.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Fish Set dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported by HPB for this dish. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this dish. Saturated fat (2.31 g/100 g), trans fat (0.08 g/100 g), and vitamin C (1.54 mg/100 g) were originally provided at confidence 0.70 from a secondary delivery-platform source in the input record; these values are retained as the best available supplementary data but should be verified against an approved primary source when possible. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate based on the weighted carbohydrate contribution of white basmati rice/pulao and naan components from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish was found. Glycemic load per 650 g serving ≈ 95.9. The portion size of 650 g is derived from the input record and should be validated against a primary source. The dish composition (Fish Curry, Dal Tadka, Saffron Pulao, Butter Naan) is taken from the authoritative input description.
